rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEON J. WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 10/5/99 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Betty Williams Petition For Partition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Betty Williams has not given proper notice of the petition for partition filed 11/30/2020 on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120. The 06/04/2021 Notice of Hearing shows notice being mailed on June 4, 2021 with the hearing date on page 1 listed as May 11, 2021. 2) Betty Williams counsel still has not provided the required courtesy copy of the petition or the required proposed order. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. 3) The Joint Statement is the filing queue is not signed or verified by parties. (CRC 7.103).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED LORD JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court may wish to inquire into the circumstances surrounding the execution of, and the consideration for, the written agreement between Kenneth Paul Jones and Probate Advance, LLC, by which Mr. Jones assigned $15,500 to Probate Advance just 19 days before this hearing for which notice was mailed to him on 8/3/21. [Probate Code Section 11604.5(g).] As a result of this last-minute assignment, Mr. Jones received just $8,000 and will receive a refund of $3,500 (as the estate will be distributed within 6 months from the date of the agreement) or a total of just $17,523.41 (ultimately) of the $21,523.41 to which he would have been entitled otherwise. (Counsel for the administrator may be asked to address the discrepancy in the amount of the reserve between paragraphs 21 and 22 and the prayer and/or how the annual bond fee is calculated.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN R. PRATT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The proof of service indicates that notice was mailed on 8/4/21, but the amended petition was not file-stamped until 8/5/21. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Additionally, likely as a result of our clerks being woefully understaffed, the amended petition was not actually E-filed until 8/18/21, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Notice of the 9/28/21 hearing of the amended petition must be given at least 15 days before that date or waivers of notice from all interested parties must be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/17/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Since notice is still being given to the beneficiaries of the pecuniary bequests and no Notice of Proposed Action has been filed, although not required to be filed, they have presumably not yet received their $10,000 bequests. [Probate Code Section 10520.] Interest at 7% has been accruing on the general pecuniary bequests since 4/13/2019. [Probate Code Sections 12003 and 12001.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DIANA IGNATIUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Again, due to the dearth of clerks, courtesy copies of recent filings have not yet been received by the Examiner other than the form DE-147S filed on 7/29/21. The "Declaration" amending Attachment 8 was file stamped 8/19/21 but not actually E-filed until 8/24/21. It was skimmed online to the extent possible and compared with the proofs of service filed on 7/6/21 and 8/16/21. Notice to a minor (even if over age 12) must also be given to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] Notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] Item 2d(2) of the petition (amount of bond) is incomplete. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/17/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
